name: First Jackson Bank, INC.
description_short: Regional community bank serving northeastern Alabama and southeastern Tennessee with six branch locations, offering personal and business banking services including checking, savings, loans, and digital banking.
description_long: First Jackson Bank is a regional community bank headquartered in Stevenson, Alabama, operating six convenient branch offices across northeastern Alabama and southeastern Tennessee. The bank has established itself as a local financial institution focused on serving the banking needs of individuals, families, and businesses in its service area. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), money market accounts, and both personal and commercial loans. For business customers, First Jackson Bank provides business checking and savings accounts along with business lending products. Digital banking capabilities include online banking, a new mobile banking app, online bill payment, telephone banking available 24 hours, and electronic statements delivered via email. The bank also operates a 24-hour ATM network throughout its service region. First Jackson Bank distinguishes itself through its regional focus and personalized customer service in a community banking model. The bank maintains direct customer support via multiple channels (phone, in-person at six locations) and implements proactive fraud prevention measures, including alerts about phishing scams and customizable debit card geographic restrictions. The institution offers overdraft privilege options and maintains relatively accessible lending programs for local customers. The bank's primary limitation is its geographic restriction to a four-state service area (Alabama, Tennessee, Georgia, Florida), with debit card purchases blocked outside these states by default—requiring customers to contact the bookkeeping department for temporary removal. As a smaller regional bank, First Jackson Bank may have more limited product offerings, technology sophistication, or competitive rates compared to larger national banking institutions. The bank's six-location footprint means it cannot provide the nationwide branch access of major banks, though this aligns with its community banking positioning.
services: Online banking and mobile banking app
pros: 24-hour ATM network available throughout northeastern Alabama and southeastern Tennessee
New mobile banking app and online banking platform for digital account management
24-hour telephone banking service for quick balance inquiries and account access
Six convenient physical branch locations for in-person banking needs
Comprehensive loan offerings for both personal and commercial customers
Online bill payment service included with accounts
Proactive fraud prevention measures including phishing alerts and customizable card restrictions
cons: Debit cards blocked for purchases outside Alabama, Tennessee, Georgia, and Florida by default, requiring customer service call to temporarily unblock
Limited to six locations—no nationwide branch network or accessibility for customers outside service area
As a regional bank, likely offers fewer product options and digital features compared to large national banks
No information provided on rates, APYs, or fee structures on publicly available website
Geographic limitation restricts growth potential and customer base expansion
best_for: Individuals and families living in northeastern Alabama or southeastern Tennessee seeking local community banking
Small business owners in the bank's service area needing business checking and commercial lending
Customers prioritizing in-person banking relationships and local customer service
Residents of the four-state service area (AL, TN, GA, FL) who rarely travel outside these states
diagnosis: First Jackson Bank is best for individuals, families, and small businesses located in northeastern Alabama or southeastern Tennessee who value community banking, personalized service, and in-person relationships with their financial institution. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—customers must live within or primarily conduct banking in the four-state region (AL, TN, GA, FL), and debit card use outside this area requires advance notification to the bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Regional community bank serving northeastern Alabama and southeastern Tennessee with personal and business banking services.
